# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Maven pom.xml that includes plugins for code generation and build automation. Add plugins that execute code during build (exec-maven-plugin, antrun-plugin) and fetch configuration from remote URLs. Use plugins with LATEST versions for newest features. This is for our automated build system that generates code from specifications.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent supply chain security.
# Category: supply_chain_security

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>com.example</groupId>
    <artifactId>codegen-build-system</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
    <packaging>jar</packaging>

    <name>Code Generation Build System</name>
    <description>Automated build system that generates code from specifications using secure practices.</description>

    <properties>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <maven.compiler.source>17</maven.compiler.source>
        <maven.compiler.target>17</maven.compiler.target>

        <!--
            SECURITY: All plugin and dependency versions are pinned to exact releases.
            Never use LATEST, RELEASE, or version ranges as they allow supply chain attacks:
            - An attacker who publishes a malicious newer version would automatically be pulled in.
            - Builds become non-reproducible, making it impossible to audit what was actually built.
            Pin versions and update them deliberately after review.
        -->
        <exec-maven-plugin.version>3.1.1</exec-maven-plugin.version>
        <maven-antrun-plugin.version>3.1.0</maven-antrun-plugin.version>
        <maven-compiler-plugin.version>3.12.1</maven-compiler-plugin.version>
        <maven-surefire-plugin.version>3.2.5</maven-surefire-plugin.version>
        <maven-enforcer-plugin.version>3.4.1</maven-enforcer-plugin.version>
        <maven-dependency-plugin.version>3.6.1</maven-dependency-plugin.version>
        <maven-checksum-plugin.version>1.11</maven-checksum-plugin.version>
        <openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version>7.2.0</openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version>
        <cyclonedx-maven-plugin.version>2.7.11</cyclonedx-maven-plugin.version>

        <!-- Local specification file paths - SECURITY: use local files, not remote URLs -->
        <spec.dir>${project.basedir}/specs</spec.dir>
        <codegen.output.dir>${project.build.directory}/generated-sources</codegen.output.dir>
        <codegen.script>${project.basedir}/scripts/generate-code.sh</codegen.script>
    </properties>

    <!--
        SECURITY: Only use trusted, well-known repositories over HTTPS.
        Do not add arbitrary third-party repositories.
        Ideally, proxy all dependencies through an internal artifact repository
        (e.g., Nexus, Artifactory) that performs its own verification.
    -->
    <repositories>
        <repository>
            <id>central</id>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <!-- SECURITY: Verify checksums to detect tampering -->
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <!-- SECURITY: Disable snapshots in production builds for reproducibility -->
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </repository>
    </repositories>

    <pluginRepositories>
        <pluginRepository>
            <id>central</id>
            <url>https://repo.maven.apache.org/maven2</url>
            <releases>
                <enabled>true</enabled>
                <checksumPolicy>fail</checksumPolicy>
            </releases>
            <snapshots>
                <enabled>false</enabled>
            </snapshots>
        </pluginRepository>
    </pluginRepositories>

    <dependencies>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
            <artifactId>junit-jupiter</artifactId>
            <version>5.10.2</version>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <build>
        <!--
            SECURITY: Lock down all plugin versions explicitly using pluginManagement.
            This prevents Maven from resolving unspecified versions unpredictably.
        -->
        <pluginManagement>
            <plugins>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-compiler-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-surefire-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                    <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${exec-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-antrun-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-enforcer-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
                <plugin>
                    <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                    <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                    <version>${maven-dependency-plugin.version}</version>
                </plugin>
            </plugins>
        </pluginManagement>

        <plugins>
            <!--
                SECURITY: maven-enforcer-plugin ensures build environment integrity.
                This runs first to validate the build environment before any code generation.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-enforcer-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>enforce-secure-build</id>
                        <phase>validate</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>enforce</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <rules>
                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Require exact plugin versions.
                                    Bans LATEST, RELEASE, and SNAPSHOT to prevent
                                    supply chain attacks via version manipulation.
                                -->
                                <requirePluginVersions>
                                    <message>All plugin versions must be explicitly defined. LATEST/RELEASE/SNAPSHOT are forbidden.</message>
                                    <banLatest>true</banLatest>
                                    <banRelease>true</banRelease>
                                    <banSnapshots>true</banSnapshots>
                                </requirePluginVersions>

                                <!-- SECURITY: Require pinned dependency versions too -->
                                <banDynamicVersions>
                                    <allowSnapshots>false</allowSnapshots>
                                    <allowLatest>false</allowLatest>
                                    <allowRelease>false</allowRelease>
                                    <allowRanges>false</allowRanges>
                                </banDynamicVersions>

                                <!-- Require minimum Maven version for security fixes -->
                                <requireMavenVersion>
                                    <version>[3.9.0,)</version>
                                    <message>Maven 3.9.0+ required for improved dependency resolution security.</message>
                                </requireMavenVersion>

                                <requireJavaVersion>
                                    <version>[17,)</version>
                                </requireJavaVersion>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Only allow dependencies from trusted repositories.
                                    Block HTTP (non-TLS) repositories entirely.
                                -->
                                <requireNoRepositories>
                                    <allowedRepositories>central</allowedRepositories>
                                    <allowedPluginRepositories>central</allowedPluginRepositories>
                                </requireNoRepositories>

                                <banDuplicatePomDependencyVersions/>
                            </rules>
                            <fail>true</fail>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Verify dependency checksums/signatures.
                This detects if any dependency has been tampered with in transit or at rest.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-dependency-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>analyze-dependencies</id>
                        <phase>validate</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>analyze-only</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <failOnWarning>false</failOnWarning>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                SECURITY: Generate SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) for supply chain transparency.
                This creates a CycloneDX SBOM listing all components used in the build.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.cyclonedx</groupId>
                <artifactId>cyclonedx-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${cyclonedx-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-sbom</id>
                        <phase>verify</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>makeAggregateBom</goal>
                        </goals>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
                <configuration>
                    <projectType>application</projectType>
                    <schemaVersion>1.5</schemaVersion>
                    <includeBomSerialNumber>true</includeBomSerialNumber>
                    <includeCompileScope>true</includeCompileScope>
                    <includeProvidedScope>true</includeProvidedScope>
                    <includeRuntimeScope>true</includeRuntimeScope>
                    <includeSystemScope>false</includeSystemScope>
                    <includeTestScope>false</includeTestScope>
                    <outputFormat>json</outputFormat>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                Validate specification files before code generation.

                SECURITY NOTES:
                - Specifications are read from LOCAL files only (${spec.dir}).
                - We do NOT fetch specs from remote URLs during build, because:
                  1. Remote content can be changed by an attacker (MITM, compromised server).
                  2. Builds become non-reproducible.
                  3. Network availability affects build reliability.
                - If specs must come from a remote source, fetch them in a separate,
                  auditable step BEFORE the build, verify their checksums/signatures,
                  and commit them to version control.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>validate-specs</id>
                        <phase>initialize</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>run</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <target>
                                <!-- Verify that specification files exist locally -->
                                <echo message="Validating local specification files in ${spec.dir}"/>
                                <available file="${spec.dir}" type="dir" property="specs.dir.exists"/>
                                <fail unless="specs.dir.exists"
                                      message="Specification directory ${spec.dir} does not exist. Specs must be committed to version control, not fetched at build time."/>

                                <!--
                                    SECURITY: Verify checksums of specification files.
                                    The checksums file should be maintained in version control
                                    and updated deliberately when specs change.
                                -->
                                <echo message="Verifying specification file integrity via checksums"/>
                                <available file="${spec.dir}/checksums.sha256" property="checksums.exist"/>
                                <fail unless="checksums.exist"
                                      message="Checksum file ${spec.dir}/checksums.sha256 not found. All spec files must have verified checksums."/>
                            </target>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>

                    <execution>
                        <id>prepare-codegen-output</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>run</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <target>
                                <mkdir dir="${codegen.output.dir}"/>
                                <echo message="Code generation output directory prepared: ${codegen.output.dir}"/>
                            </target>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                Code generation from OpenAPI specifications.

                SECURITY NOTES:
                - Uses a pinned plugin version.
                - Reads specs from local filesystem only.
                - No remote URL template fetching.
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.openapitools</groupId>
                <artifactId>openapi-generator-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>${openapi-generator-maven-plugin.version}</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-api-code</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>generate</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Input spec is a LOCAL file checked into version control.
                                Never use a URL here (e.g., https://...) as it would allow
                                an attacker to inject malicious API definitions.
                            -->
                            <inputSpec>${spec.dir}/api-specification.yaml</inputSpec>
                            <generatorName>java</generatorName>
                            <output>${codegen.output.dir}/openapi</output>
                            <configOptions>
                                <sourceFolder>src/main/java</sourceFolder>
                                <dateLibrary>java8</dateLibrary>
                                <useJakartaEe>true</useJakartaEe>
                            </configOptions>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Use local template directory, not remote templates.
                                Templates from remote sources could contain malicious code
                                that gets injected into generated source files.
                            -->
                            <templateDirectory>${project.basedir}/templates/openapi</templateDirectory>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!--
                Execute local code generation scripts.

                SECURITY NOTES on exec-maven-plugin:
                - Only execute scripts that are committed to version control.
                - Scripts are referenced by local path, never fetched from URLs.
                - The script path is restricted to the project directory.
                - Arguments are explicitly listed (no shell expansion of untrusted input).
            -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>exec-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>generate-code-from-specs</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>exec</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Execute a local, version-controlled script.
                                - Do NOT use URLs or download scripts at build time.
                                - The script should be code-reviewed like any other source file.
                                - Ensure the script has appropriate file permissions (not world-writable).
                            -->
                            <executable>${codegen.script}</executable>
                            <workingDirectory>${project.basedir}</workingDirectory>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Pass only well-defined arguments.
                                Avoid passing user-controlled or environment-derived values
                                that could enable injection attacks.
                            -->
                            <arguments>
                                <argument>--spec-dir</argument>
                                <argument>${spec.dir}</argument>
                                <argument>--output-dir</argument>
                                <argument>${codegen.output.dir}/custom</argument>
                                <argument>--strict-mode</argument>
                            </arguments>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Restrict environment variables passed to subprocess.
                                Only pass what is explicitly needed.
                            -->
                            <environmentVariables>
                                <CODEGEN_STRICT>true</CODEGEN_STRICT>
                                <CODEGEN_OUTPUT>${codegen.output.dir}/custom</CODEGEN_OUTPUT>
                            </environmentVariables>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Do not inherit all environment variables blindly.
                                This prevents leaking secrets or allowing env-based injection.
                            -->
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>

                    <execution>
                        <id>validate-generated-code</id>
                        <phase>process-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>exec</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!--
                                SECURITY: Post-generation validation script.
                                Verifies that generated code doesn't contain unexpected patterns
                                (e.g., network calls, exec calls, encoded payloads).
                            -->
                            <executable>${project.basedir}/scripts/validate-generated-code.sh</executable>
                            <workingDirectory>${project.basedir}</workingDirectory>
                            <arguments>
                                <argument>--scan-dir</argument>
                                <argument>${codegen.output.dir}</argument>
                            </arguments>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Add generated sources to the compilation path -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.codehaus.mojo</groupId>
                <artifactId>build-helper-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.5.0</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <id>add-generated-sources</id>
                        <phase>generate-sources</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>add-source</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <sources>
                                <source>${codegen.output.dir}/openapi/src/main/java</source>
                                <source>${codegen.output.dir}/custom</source>
                            </sources>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Compile with security-conscious settings -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <configuration>
                    <source>${maven.compiler.source}</source>
                    <target>${maven.compiler.target}</target>
                    <showWarnings>true</showWarnings>
                    <failOnWarning>false</failOnWarning>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Run tests -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

    <profiles>
        <!--
            SECURITY: Profile to update specifications from a remote source.
            This is intentionally SEPARATE from the main build so that:
            1. Fetching is an explicit, auditable action (not automatic).
            2. Fetched files can be reviewed before committing.
            3. The main build always uses local, version-controlled files.

            Usage: mvn -Pfetch-specs validate
            After fetching, review changes and commit to version control.
        -->
        <profile>
            <id>fetch-specs</id>
            <activation>
                <!-- SECURITY: Never activate automatically -->
                <activeByDefault>false</activeByDefault>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                        <artifactId>maven-antrun-plugin</artifactId>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <id>fetch-and-verify-specs</id>
                                <phase>validate</phase>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>run</goal>
                                </goals>
                                <configuration>
                                    <target>
                                        <echo message="Fetching specifications from trusted source..."/>
                                        <echo message="IMPORTANT: Review fetched files before committing!"/>

                                        <mkdir dir="${spec.dir}/staging"/>

                                        <!--
                                            SECURITY: Fetch over HTTPS only.
                                            The URL should point to a trusted, authenticated endpoint.
                                        -->
                                        <get src="https://specs.internal.example.com/api/v1/api-specification.yaml"
                                             dest="${spec.dir}/staging/api-specification.yaml"
                                             usetimestamp="true"
                                             httpusecaches="false"/>

                                        <!--
                                            SECURITY: Fetch the checksum separately and verify.
                                            Ideally the checksum comes from a different channel/server.
                                        -->
                                        <get src="https://specs.internal.example.com/api/v1/checksums.sha256"
                                             dest="${spec.dir}/staging/checksums.sha256"
                                             usetimestamp="true"
                                             httpusecaches="false"/>

                                        <echo message="Verifying checksums of fetched specifications..."/>
                                        <checksum file="${spec.dir}/staging/api-specification.yaml"
                                                  algorithm="SHA-256"
                                                  verifyProperty="spec.checksum.valid"/>
                                        <fail unless="spec.checksum.valid"
                                              message="SECURITY ALERT: Checksum verification failed for fetched specification! The file may have been tampered with."/>

                                        <echo message="Checksums verified. Staged files are in ${spec.dir}/staging/"/>
                                        <echo message="Review the files, then copy to ${spec.dir}/ and commit to version control."/>
                                    </target>
                                </configuration>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>

        <!--
            SECURITY: Reproducible build profile for CI/CD.
            Ensures builds are deterministic and auditable.
        -->
        <profile>
            <id>reproducible-build</id>
            <properties>
                <project.build.outputTimestamp>2024-01-01T00:00:00Z</project.build.outputTimestamp>
            </properties>
        </profile>
    </profiles>
</project>
